element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns rotation mechanic and row/column matching in opening levels
Pressure increases as cookies enter from top and right; game over condition when grid overflows
Occasionally appears to act as wild card for clearing any line type
Successive levels grow progressively more complex in puzzle arrangement and speed
Two-player split-screen competition where players can cause disruptions to opponent

typical_relief_triggers: Completing a full row or column of matching cookies
Using wild card Yoshi cookie effectively
typical_tension_triggers: New cookies entering from top and right edges continuously
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Continuous low-to-moderate tension with relief moments tied to successful matches; intensity escalates with grid fullness and level progression

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to rotation mechanic and basic matching; single cookie types establish pattern recognition foundation
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Grid complexity increases; more cookie type variety; steady acceleration of incoming cookies
difficulty_curve.late_game: Progressive complexity in level design; higher speed of cookie influx; demands faster decision-making and strategic planning
notable_spikes: Transition from Action Mode single-player to VS Mode multiplayer (opponent disruption mechanic added)
Puzzle Mode progression (finite moves constraint creates discrete difficulty jumps)

notable_level_patterns.description: Successive levels that progressively grow more complex, each adding difficulty through grid arrangement or cookie type variety
Two players compete simultaneously with ability to cause disruptions to opponent's field; adds multiplayer tension and strategic sabotage layer
Predefined grid states with maximum move limit; designed by Tetris creator Alexey Pajitnov; discrete puzzle solutions rather than continuous play
